QF.id: VI Depository and its Business Partners_8

question.text: An applicant for DP registration should not have been convicted in any matter involving misappropriation of funds in the immediately preceding:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The applicant should not have been convicted in any of the five years immediately preceding the filing of the application in any matter involving misappropriation, theft, embezzlement, fraud, or forgery. **The Trap:** 'Three years' is the period after which an expelled/barred person MAY be considered at the depository's discretion. Don't confuse the conviction bar (5 years absolute) with the expulsion bar (3 years discretionary). **Cross-Exam Context:** Series III-A covers the 'fit and proper' criteria for market intermediaries. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** 5 years for conviction (absolute bar), 3 years for expulsion/suspension (discretionary, depository may still consider).
